ai_summary.interview_process_and_format: The interview process is generally multi-staged, often including initial screenings, technical rounds, and live coding tasks.
ai_summary.candidate_experience: While some candidates found the interviewers to be supportive and professional, others reported inconsistent or negative experiences.
ai_summary.interview_timelines: Communication about progress can be inconsistent. Several candidates highlighted delays in receiving feedback.

faqs.is_it_hard_to_get_hired: Glassdoor users rated their interview experience at Revolut as 35.4% positive with an average difficulty rating.

interviews.title: Senior Java Developer Interview
interviews.interview_description: Smooth polished process. Average tech level of interviewers. Narrow minded persons. Probably their main criteria is speed of coding, not code quality.

questions.question: Implement money transfer method for java library.

interviews.title: Senior Java Developer Interview
interviews.interview_description: A lot of stages including live coding tasks, common engineering problems discussion and system design.

questions.question: Build a top-level design for a system responsible for temporary debit card issuance.

interviews.title: Senior Java Software Engineer Interview
interviews.interview_description: The overall interview process was well-structured and professional, consisting of four stages over a period of time.

questions.question: Implement LoadBalancer using TDD style with Random/RoundRobin strategies.
Implement a thread-safe Account Ledger given a skeleton.
System Design an Apartment Booking application with a 3rd party API integration.
Describe a project you delivered end-to-end in depth, focus on innovation and initiative.

interviews.title: Senior Java Developer Interview
interviews.interview_description: HR Call – Initial screening with HR discussing background, experience, and motivation. Followed by technical rounds.

questions.question: One of the questions was about concurrent collections and their usage.

interviews.title: Senior Java Developer Interview
interviews.interview_description: 1. HR call 2. Live coding + some questions about complexity of the solution (Load balancer). Decent experience overall.

questions.question: Live coding + questions about complexity of the solution. Load balancer with some extra requirements added once implemented.

interviews.title: Senior Java Developer Interview
interviews.interview_description: Had an intro call with a recruitment coordinator that went well. Was assured Revolut has a good culture. Company makes a strong accent on concurrency initially.

questions.question: Create a load balancer with production quality considerations. Concurrency-focused task.

interviews.title: Senior Java Developer Interview
interviews.interview_description: A 20-minute interview with HR where you describe your experience and answer simple questions about Java and data structures.

questions.question: Can elements be not equal if they have the same hashCode? What is the time complexity to get an element from a HashMap? What transaction levels exist in databases?

interviews.title: Senior Java Developer Interview
interviews.interview_description: Simple intro about the company and position and high-level questions about Java design patterns.

questions.question: Talk about the Memento pattern.

interviews.title: Senior Java Developer Interview
interviews.interview_description: Interview on Zoom with two engineers from Berlin and Moscow, conducted in English. A live coding session was part of the interview.

questions.question: Question about transfer between two accounts – how to achieve consistency and avoid double-spending money.

interviews.title: Senior Java Developer Interview
interviews.interview_description: Very common questions. A lot of questions related to databases even for a Java developer role. Answering anything below the expected level means failure.

questions.question: ACID principles. How to make a transaction in a database.

interviews.title: Senior Java Developer Interview
interviews.interview_description: Just a single call – a quick technical screening call; declined afterwards. Questions seemed standard.

questions.question: Describe a given pattern in microservices architectures.

interviews.title: Senior Software Engineer - Java Developer Interview
interviews.interview_description: 1. Introductory call with a recruiter – common questions plus some simple technical questions. 2. Technical interview with engineers.

questions.question: Lots of questions about concurrent and multithreaded programming, race conditions, deadlock, and optimization of relational databases.

interviews.title: Senior Java Developer Interview
interviews.interview_description: First HR interview, ~10 min. ~5 min explaining company processes and how the team works, followed by a few technical questions.

questions.question: What's the time complexity of a lookup in a HashMap?
Explain what is a transaction?

interviews.title: Senior Java Developer Interview
interviews.interview_description: 1. Recruiter interview – default HR interview with some basic technical questions. 2. Technical interview with engineers.

questions.question: What is a database transaction?
List database transaction isolation levels.

interviews.title: Senior Java Developer Interview
interviews.interview_description: The recruiter was described as incompetent. Interview ended when the candidate began explaining why a certain answer might be incomplete.

questions.question: How can we implement concurrency in Java?

interviews.title: Senior Java Developer Interview
interviews.interview_description: Described as the most inadequate interview in 11 years of career. Despite many negative reviews, Revolut continues with the same process.

questions.question: Write something based on abstract requirements.

interviews.title: Senior Java Developer Interview
interviews.interview_description: First round was a pleasant talk with HR. HR asked several basic technical questions and a coding interview challenge was included.

questions.question: Write some concurrent code. How could we implement this using Java concurrency mechanisms? Using database locking mechanisms?

interviews.title: Senior Java Developer Interview
interviews.interview_description: 1-hour coding interview where you must build an architecture based on abstract requirements. Speed of implementation is assessed.

questions.question: Write an application based on abstract requirements.

interviews.title: Senior Java Developer Interview
interviews.interview_description: Friendly talk with recruiter, followed by a technical interview scheduled the next day with an unexpectedly senior interviewer.

questions.question: Computational complexity of data structures.

interviews.title: Senior Java Developer Interview
interviews.interview_description: Convenient and interesting HR round with basic Java questions; simple but complex task in a live coding session.

questions.question: Working with random String generators.
CQRS (Command Query Responsibility Segregation).
Internal database data structures.
TDD (Test-Driven Development).

interviews.title: Senior Java Developer Interview
interviews.interview_description: First step is a home assignment: a small RESTful service about banking with vague requirements. Followed by a live coding interview.

questions.question: Design a RESTful service about banking (requirements intentionally vague/abstract).
